headline: Agent orchestration at scale dominated the month as teams pushed from single agents to fleets coordinated through shared protocols.
body: System architecture was the standout mover, climbing on momentum 145 as multi-agent tooling matured: Cursor described running 128 coding agents at once with inter-agent messaging and review, while WorkOS detailed agents dispatched to isolated git worktrees with verification gates and voice briefs. Model Context Protocol threaded through the window, from Looker and ADK tutorials to the proposed WebMCP standard that exposes typed website tools to agents on Chrome 146. Memory systems and KV cache quantization broke out as the supporting infrastructure for long-running and parallel agent workloads. New entrants including text-to-CAD, faithful generation, and router-prompt co-evolution surfaced at the edges, alongside continued evaluation of Anthropic's Fable 5.
